message:
🌱 Add linkerd: Support RSA certificates from external PKI mission (#1652)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "linkerd-4323-support-rsa-certificates-from-external-pki",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "linkerd: Support RSA certificates from external PKI",
+    "description": "Support RSA certificates from external PKI. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current linkerd deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your linkerd version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nhelm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working linkerd installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review linkerd configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nkubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd\n```\n## Feature Request\nWe would like to onboard to using an external CA, but unfortunately they do not support ECDSA, only RSA:\n```\nError: failed to read CA from linkerd-identity-issuer: must use ECDSA for public key algorithm, instead RSA was"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support RSA certificates from external PKI",
+        "description": "Sorry, I mean the Key and CSR. The question is can the ECDSA CSR be signed by the RSA issuer key? If that works, I think we could relax some of the service-side checks. We'd need to validate that the proxies can still verify the certs, obviously; and it would be helpful to understand how the\n```yaml\nError: failed to read CA from linkerd-identity-issuer: must use ECDSA for public key algorithm, instead RSA was used\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd\nkubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support RSA certificates from external PKI\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Sorry, I mean the Key and CSR. The question is can the ECDSA CSR be signed by the RSA issuer key? If that works, I think we could relax some of the service-side checks. We'd need to validate that the proxies can still verify the certs, obviously; and it would be helpful to understand how the resulting certs differ (especially in terms of size).",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Error: failed to read CA from linkerd-identity-issuer: must use ECDSA for public key algorithm, instead RSA was used",
+        "[     0.107075474s]  INFO linkerd2_proxy: Admin interface on 0.0.0.0:4191\n[     0.107157548s]  INFO linkerd2_proxy: Inbound interface on 0.0.0.0:4143\n[     0.107175806s]  INFO linkerd2_proxy: Outbound interface on 127.0.0.1:4140\n[     0.107188262s]  INFO linkerd2_proxy: Tap interface on 0.0.0.0:4190\n[     0.107199953s]  INFO linkerd2_proxy: Local identity is linkerd-controller.linkerd.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local\n[     0.107229214s]  INFO linkerd2_proxy: Identity verified via linkerd-identity.linkerd.svc.cluster.local:8080 (linkerd-identity.linkerd.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local)\n[     0.107244395s]  INFO linkerd2_proxy: Destinations resolved via linkerd-dst.linkerd.svc.cluster.local:8086 (linkerd-destination.linkerd.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.l"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
